content: i am sock to heart that the hot - reload not working in flutter web

guys don't use flutter for web apps. Here are the problems i faced in web during my 3 years experience with flutter
1. No hot reload, for every single piece of change, you need to check from starting
2. No deep hyperlinking. Not seo friendly. Unable to deep link to a page properly
3. Many plugins are not properly implemented in web
4. Initial page load time is higher than pure html or other frameworks
5. responsive page building is difficult
6. after each update of your website, in case of pure html you only need to download new changes, all other style files, js files automatically cached, while in the case of flutter, you need to download whole main.js file
7. if you already use a framework in backend, then its easy to integrate your frontend logic using tempate like blade in laravel, handlebars in nodejs. While in flutter everything is need to build as APIs in backend.
8. Testing is difficult, in pure html you can manually test individual pages

> @michaellee8 I'm not aware of any published articles. The best technical deep dive is to check out the [code for the dart2wasm compiler](https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/tree/main/pkg/dart2wasm).
> 
> The Dart VM isn't compiled to WASM. Instead, the compiler uses the new WasmGC instructions that are being added to WebAssembly spec right now (it's a [stage 3 proposal](https://github.com/WebAssembly/proposals) as of this writing), so the WebAssembly runtime _is_ the VM for Dart when compiled to wasm. Hot-reload depends on the code loading capabilities of the WebAssembly runtime, and at the moment those capabilities are too limited to implement a Dart VM-like hot reload. For the time being we'll continue supporting hot restart via DDC and JavaScript.
> 
> We've mused about different kinds of approaches we could take to add stateful hot reload:
> 
> * Add hot reload to DDC.
> * A [Dartium](https://google.fandom.com/wiki/Dartium)-like approach of embedding the Dart VM into Chromium.
> * Use Flutter's desktop embedder, perhaps with Chromium attached to it to have access to browser API.
> * Propose a hot-reload extension to WebAssembly.
> 
> I'm sure there are other options we haven't even imagined yet. What we do know is that it would be a huge effort, so we really need to balance it with other things our users want us to do.

It may be huge effort, but for web development, improved performance, using fancy wasm etc. is not as important as we are forced to pick other framework.
why my comment is mark as duplicate ?
there is truth flutter not enough good flutter web

also i read this comment and currently it's says it's duplicate it's not but it's truth which flutter team have to accept 

'''
guys don't use flutter for web apps. Here are the problems i faced in web during my 3 years experience with flutter

No hot reload, for every single piece of change, you need to check from starting
No deep hyperlinking. Not seo friendly. Unable to deep link to a page properly
Many plugins are not properly implemented in web
Initial page load time is higher than pure html or other frameworks
responsive page building is difficult
after each update of your website, in case of pure html you only need to download new changes, all other style files, js files automatically cached, while in the case of flutter, you need to download whole main.js file
if you already use a framework in backend, then its easy to integrate your frontend logic using tempate like blade in laravel, handlebars in nodejs. While in flutter everything is need to build as APIs in backend.
Testing is difficult, in pure html you can manually test individual pages

'''

above comment is almost true .
Thank you .

Before commenting on Flutter issues please see our [comment policy](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Issue-hygiene#comments) and [code of conduct](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

Notably, please do not add comments to the effect of "me too" (just click the "thumb-up" button at the top of the issue), and please do not act as if you expect other people to fix bugs for you. Flutter is an open source project with many contributors (some paid, some not). If there is an issue that is blocking your work, rather than expecting someone else to fix it for you, consider how you might be able to contribute to the project to resolve it. If the problem is outside your skillset, you may be able to hire someone to fix the bug for you.

Similarly, please do not ask for updates. Any updates to issues will be reported when they are available. In the meantime, for the most [popular issues](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Popular-issues), the comment at the top of the issue will often have a link to a comment with the current status.
any update ?
i think team is not working on flutter web .
flutter app development is just awesome but web we need same simple as app development .
Where can I start to look to contribute to Flutter + add stateful hot reload?

I wonder if, with the advent of WebAssembly (Wasm) support for Flutter Web, this couldn't be more easily achievable because it wouldn't depend entirely on JavaScript.

Another idea would be running the debug app like an emulator on top of a JavaScript version of the Dart VM.
Any update on this? Any work in progress or something like that?

@tomasweigenast just want to point toward the Flutter roadmap for 2024: https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Roadmap
It's listed there as the last item with "plan to resume work".

Roadmaps can change, but it seems like we can carefully hope for some progress this year (if everything goes well).
Feel free to react instead of responding to not spam this issue 😌

Indeed - it's part of the Dart web team plans to dive into hot reload support. It's a long road ahead and we are just getting started, so we don't have much to share at this time. We'll be tracking our efforts in https://github.com/dart-lang/sdk/issues/54934.
This issue should be tagged as P1 and not as P2.
Please see <https://github.com/flutter/flutter/wiki/Issue-hygiene#escalating-an-issue-that-has-the-wrong-priority>.
We are almost at the end of the year, could you please advise if the work for hot reload in Web has been resumed?

@ElihuJ Apparently YES, something like https://github.com/flutter/flutter/pull/161276 was merged recently which brings flutter closer to hot reload, though I didn't yet find anything organized as a roadmap to display what's implemented and what's not to arrive to fully implemented hot reload.
Thanks for your patience. It will be rewarded. 😀

For those who missed the update - few hours ago @kevmoo announced on reddit that this feature is currently available on the beta channel - Requesting everyone to try it and file for any discovered issues as per the post's guidelines.

Full post on reddit can be [accessed here](https://www.reddit.com/r/FlutterDev/comments/1jedakr/try_out_hot_reload_on_the_web_with_the_latest/).

A big shoutout to @kevmoo to finally make this happen 🫡 🙏. Fingers crossed for the stable release 🤞.

> A big shoutout to @kevmoo 

I'm just the joyful messenger. We have an AMAZING team who has pulled this all together.

I'm using beta with --wasm and getting this error, 

```Failed to Hot Reload: DebugAdapterException: LateInitializationError: Local 'report' has not been initialized.``` 

now when I save a file I need to wait almost 1 minute to app to refresh, without wasm it seems to work, but it still reloads the whole page

Hi all,

We're happy to announce that the newly released Flutter 3.32 now includes hot reload for the web! 

There are some pretty significant changes to our web development compiler so we've included an opt-in flag for the feature. This will allow developers to kick the tires on it before we [enable it by default](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/167510) in the next release.

Check out the [Flutter docs](https://docs.flutter.dev/platform-integration/web/building#hot-reload-web) for more information on how to enable this feature in your local environment.

Happy hot reloading 🔥 ♻ !

- The Dart Web team

> Hi all,
> 
> We're happy to announce that the newly released Flutter 3.32 now includes hot reload for the web!
> 
> There are some pretty significant changes to our web development compiler so we've included an opt-in flag for the feature. This will allow developers to kick the tires on it before we [enable it by default](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/167510) in the next release.
> 
> Check out the [Flutter docs](https://docs.flutter.dev/platform-integration/web/building#hot-reload-web) for more information on how to enable this feature in your local environment.
> 
> Happy hot reloading 🔥 ♻ !
> 
> * The Dart Web team

Ah finally! I've been waiting a long time for this (years?). Congratulations to the team!
I've addressed the comments in the design doc, this PR is ready for review.

## [shared_preferences] Android exception: `Unable to establish connection on channel: "dev.flutter.pigeon.shared_preferences_android.SharedPreferencesApi.getAll"`

Long explanation of what is happening https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153075#issuecomment-3313363609 

TLDR: Update your Android Gradle Plugin version to 8.9.1 or higher and ensure that all of your android plugins are using at least AGP 8.9.1 or higher. 

If you have a reproduction that is using AGP 8.9.1 and higher for an app and all of its plugins please add a comment with your reproduction. 

### Steps to reproduce

1. Use old api of shared preferences.
2. Build the app.

### Expected results

It should work with old api of shared preferences.

### Actual results

Getting exception as below

Unhandled Exception: PlatformException(channel-error, Unable to establish connection on channel: "dev.flutter.pigeon.shared_preferences_android.SharedPreferencesApi.getAll"., null, null)

### Code sample

<details open><summary>Code sample</summary>

```dart
_sharedPrefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance();
```

</details>


### Screenshots or Video

<details open>
<summary>Screenshots / Video demonstration</summary>

[Upload media here]

</details>


### Logs

<details open><summary>Logs</summary>

```console
[Paste your logs here]
```

</details>


### Flutter Doctor output

```console
[✓] Flutter (Channel stable, 3.22.3, on macOS 14.4.1 23E224 darwin-arm64, locale en-IN)
[✓] Android toolchain - develop for Android devices (Android SDK version 34.0.0)

```

@stuartmorgan There are several reports for this issue, I wonder if this is also similar to https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153412 and is duplicated of https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/43785 per your comment at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153412#issuecomment-2299652675

No, this is unrelated to 43785; `shared_preferences` doesn't have any instance-based channels.

Given that this is only affecting release Android builds, it feels like an R8 issue. It may be that the analysis is incorrectly determining that the legacy plugin class is unreachable and removing it.

For those experiencing this:
- Does using the new `WithCache` API work in the same build configuration where the legacy API is failing?
- Does [adding a ProGuard rule](https://developer.android.com/build/shrink-code) to keep `io.flutter.plugins.sharedpreferences.LegacySharedPreferencesPlugin` fix the issue?

If this is the problem, we can add a rule to the plugin itself.
@stuartmorgan I created a new issue in that package. In my case I can sort out R8, can you explain me what you mean with the `WithCache` API?

> I created a new issue in that package.

Packages using `shared_preferences` do not need to do anything different than they were doing before.

> In my case I can sort out R8, can you explain me what you mean with the `WithCache` API?

The question is whether `SharedPreferencesWithCache.create` works in the same builds where `SharedPreferences.getInstance` fails (which would potentially indicate that R8 is stripping out the secondary Java class, but not the primary one).

Keeping this issue open for further input from others. If anyone experiences this issue, please take a look at https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/153075#issuecomment-2301620868 and confirm info and solution there.

**@stuartmorgan said:**
